title: blazewrit harness — Triage step structured_output (M1 = SHAPE)
description: structured_output schema for the TRIAGE step's OUTPUT, enforced at token-generation (grammar-constrained generation; §16). Triage is a STATELESS classification function: (input)->(output), one invocation = one output, no persistence inside the step (the OUTPUT still persists to Postgres step_runs jsonb per §5, but Triage itself reads/writes no disk and holds no flow state). The output is a 4-TYPE EXHAUSTIVE DISCRIMINATED UNION keyed on `result`: proceed | none | ambiguous | error. Grammar forces exactly one branch with that branch's required fields, so the model cannot emit a proceed without a flow_type, an error without a reason enum, etc. There are NO measured counts in this step — complexity_signal is an ENUM (high|medium|low|none), deliberately NOT a CountClaim (Triage does no code analysis, runs no tools, so it has no raw_stdout to hash). There are NO tool-dependent fields, so NO Measured|Omitted degrade union appears here (Triage invokes no degradable tool). matched_rows are signal-table SIGNAL-ROW ids — §5 RowRefs (Postgres row ids), never file paths. M2 validator contracts (TRUTH, not grammar) are listed in x-validator-contract annotations and in the step manifest; grammar gives shape only.

result.description: DISCRIMINANT for the 4-type exhaustive output union. Exactly one branch is selected by this enum; the matching oneOf branch fixes that branch's required fields.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 1 — proceed: a single flow_type was classified (signal-table single row match, or strict superset, P0-over-BugFix, or verb/target>=2 => compound).
oneOf.title: proceed

classification_metadata.description: How the classification was reached. matched_rows are signal-table row refs; confidence + complexity_signal are enums (NOT counts).

matched_rows.description: The signal-table SIGNAL-ROW id(s) this classification matched. These are signal-table LITERAL ids (S0..S16) from the Triage Signal Table — append-only string ids naming WHICH signal row matched (e.g. 'S1','S16'), NOT Postgres step_runs RowRefs and NEVER file paths. A single-row match (or strict superset) yields confidence=high; compound (verb/target>=2) may list 2+ rows. Empty is not allowed for a proceed — a proceed must cite at least one matched signal row (the basis for the flow_type).

confidence.$comment: Shared Confidence enum (high|medium|low) — all three are reachable, legitimately-produced proceed values. high = exactly 1 signal row matched (or strict superset). medium = matched but input mildly ambiguous (clarifications used to confirm a single row). low = dominant-but-weak proceed: a single dominant row classifies, but the match is weak — either prior_evidence supports the SAME flow_type yet the current input alone is marginal, OR a reclassify (reclassify_count>=1) narrowly corrects the prior classification. low signals downstream (esp. Investigate) that re-examination room remains; it is NOT the no-dominant-row case (that stays ambiguous).

complexity_signal.$comment: Shared ComplexitySignal enum (high|medium|low|none). Inferred task complexity feeding Step Depth Policy's deepen trigger. ENUM by design — NOT a CountClaim: Triage runs no tool and reads no code, so there is no raw_stdout to hash; this is a judgement signal, not a measured count.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 2 — none: input is not actionable (no concrete target / discussion / casual). Host LLM continues free conversation; A2A returns INTENT_NOT_ACTIONABLE.
oneOf.title: none

reasoning.description: Why the input is not actionable (no verb/target extracted, open brainstorming, casual exchange). Free text — channel handlers (user_session free-talk vs A2A INTENT_NOT_ACTIONABLE vs CI trigger-config error) consume it.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 3 — ambiguous: multiple signal rows matched with no superset (disposition=clarify), OR reclassify_count>=3 forced terminal escalation (disposition=escalate). The REQUIRED `disposition` enum is THE real discriminant closing H1 — terminal escalation is now an enum FIELD the orchestrator routes on (clarify -> re-invoke loop; escalate -> terminal halt), not a magic question string-match. Carries exactly ONE routing-blocking question (clarify) or one-line escalate reason (escalate).
oneOf.title: ambiguous

disposition.$comment: Shared Disposition enum (clarify|escalate). clarify = multiple non-superset rows; orchestrator surfaces `question` and re-invokes (loop). escalate = terminal: reclassify cap reached (reclassify_count>=3) or otherwise undecidable/upstream-deficient; orchestrator HALTS the flow and escalates, no re-invoke (loop prevention). This is the real enum discriminant H1/principle-3 depends on.

question.description: ONE routing-blocking clarification question when disposition=clarify (priority: verb > target > urgency > concern_count), OR a one-line escalation reason when disposition=escalate. When reclassify_count>=3 the input-field cap rule FORCES disposition=escalate (the reclassify-cap path emits ambiguous{disposition:escalate}) — orchestrator then halts the flow and escalates (no re-invoke; loop prevention). The cap-forced escalation is an INPUT-driven rule the grammar cannot see (it depends on the injected reclassify_count); it is an M2 validator_contract gating disposition, not a schema keyword.

oneOf.$comment: BRANCH 4 — error: GIBBERISH (parse failure / meaningless input) or INTERNAL (Triage self-failure). reason is a fixed enum.
oneOf.title: error

reason.description: Error cause enum. GIBBERISH = primary_input could not be parsed / is meaningless (Comprehend failed). INTERNAL = Triage itself failed.

FlowType.$comment: The 16 flow types from the Triage Signal Table. The canonical machine source is the flow-file STEMS at legacy/.blazewrit/flows/<id>.md, which use HYPHENS (bugfix-p0, bugfix-unreproducible, plan-standalone; all others single-word). This enum is the generated projection of those stems; README/DECISIONS §10 display names are human-facing. P0 and Unreproducible are DISTINCT Bug Fix variants (separate signal-table rows / separate flow files), not the same flow. 'none'/free-conversation is NOT a flow_type — it is the separate `result:none` branch, so it is intentionally absent from this enum.
FlowType.description: Triage flow_type — one of the 16 first-class flows the Signal Table maps to. Compound = verb/target>=2 (multiple blockers / multi-phase). Used only inside the proceed branch.

SignalRowId.$comment: Signal-table literal row id (Triage Signal Table). S1..S16 map to the 16 flow_types (proceed); S0 ('No actionable signal') is the non-flow row that maps to result=none. These are append-only signal-table ids, NOT Postgres step_runs RowRefs and NOT file paths.
SignalRowId.description: A Triage Signal Table row id, S0..S16. Used in classification_metadata.matched_rows to name which signal row(s) the classification matched.

x-validator-contract.rule: Each element is a signal-table LITERAL id (S0..S16), NOT a Postgres step_runs row. M2: assert each id resolves to an existing Signal Table row; grammar enforces only the S-id string shape (pattern), not row existence.

R-cap / reclassify cap: when the orchestrator-injected reclassify_count >= 3, the output MUST be result=ambiguous with question == 'reclassify cap reached, manual intervention required' (no classification attempt). reclassify_count is an INPUT the grammar never sees, so this forced-escalation is a deterministic code check on (input, output), NOT a schema keyword.

The emitted flow_type must be consistent with the matched signal rows (the signal->flow mapping in the Signal Table). compound requires verb/target>=2 (>=2 distinct matched signal intents); bugfix_p0 wins over bugfix on strict superset. Grammar fixes the enum value but cannot verify it matches the cited rows — M2 re-derives flow_type from matched_rows.

confidence=high requires exactly one matched signal row (or a strict superset). If multiple non-superset rows matched, the correct output is the ambiguous branch, not proceed(low). M2 cross-checks confidence against the match cardinality.
